comment: Biology curriculum manifest. Hand-authored seed lessons grounding Athena's biological-systems register: cells, living systems, heredity, evolution, ecology, physiology, and microbiology. Companion to data/math_corpus/ and data/canonical_corpus/. CE-13 (biology curriculum). All lessons are hand-authored, second-person teaching voice, anchored in everyday examples (a healing scratch, why bread rises, why kids look like their parents, tide pools, sourdough fermentation) before any abstract formulation. No Project Gutenberg entries — better to ship 0 dangling references than to guess at IDs.

registers.cell_intro: what a cell is, organelles intuitively, division; Stage 2
registers.living_systems: needs of life: water, food, energy, reproduction; plants vs. animals; Stage 2
registers.genetics_intro: heredity, traits, DNA as information; Stage 2-3
registers.evolution_intro: variation, selection, descent with modification; Stage 3
registers.physiology_intro: body systems: circulatory, nervous, immune; Stage 3
registers.microbiology_intro: bacteria, viruses, fermentation, gut microbiome intuition; Stage 3

works.title: What it means to be alive
Why children look like their parents
Plants and animals: two ways of being alive
Ecology: the study of living together
The body as a system of systems
The unseen majority: bacteria, viruses, and the small life around us
The immune system: a defense that learns
